reasoning: Conditioned on a Trump victory, inflation is widely expected to face upward pressure due to his proposed policies, including universal tariffs, stricter immigration enforcement, and potential pressure on the Federal Reserve to cut rates. Given that inflation has been sticky above 2.5% recently, achieving a rate below this threshold in 2025 under these inflationary policies would realistically require a significant economic slowdown or a recession. The baseline probability of a recession in any given year is around 15-20%, which provides a floor for this outcome.
